prompts.title: Prompt Index
prompts.use_when: you need the fastest file-based entry point into the prompt kit
prompts.avoid_when: the user already named the exact prompt files to load
prompts.owner_intent: Provide the lowest-friction map for loading only the minimum useful prompt context.

prompts.title: Session Start Cheatsheet
prompts.use_when: you need a quick decision aid for choosing a task prompt or deciding whether to start a new session
prompts.avoid_when: the session already has a clear, stable task prompt
prompts.owner_intent: Reduce session churn and prompt sprawl by steering users to one clear work mode.

prompts.title: Master Session Template
prompts.use_when: you want a structured handoff or repeatable session start template
prompts.avoid_when: a lightweight prompt reference is enough
prompts.owner_intent: Provide a copy-ready session scaffold for higher-discipline handoffs.

prompts.title: General Session Policy Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need the default engineering behavior for most coding sessions
prompts.avoid_when: you are only looking for a task-specific template without baseline operating rules
prompts.owner_intent: Set the baseline QA, safety, and execution rules without forcing unnecessary repo exploration.

prompts.title: Project Governance Prompt
prompts.use_when: you want stronger senior-engineer ownership and release-safety behavior
prompts.avoid_when: the task is trivial and does not need a stronger governance overlay
prompts.owner_intent: Push the model toward defensible decisions and explicit risk ownership.

prompts.title: Coding Standards Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need local-pattern-preserving implementation standards
prompts.avoid_when: the task is purely planning or non-code process work
prompts.owner_intent: Keep changes small, readable, and consistent with the surrounding codebase.

prompts.title: GitHub Workflow Prompt
prompts.use_when: you want default Git, PR, and review workflow expectations
prompts.avoid_when: Git or review workflow is out of scope for the task
prompts.owner_intent: Keep Git usage safe, reviewable, and compatible with a normal team workflow.

prompts.title: Credentials And Secrets Prompt
prompts.use_when: the work touches secrets, credentials, tokens, keys, or sensitive configs
prompts.avoid_when: the task is clearly unrelated to secrets handling
prompts.owner_intent: Force least-exposure habits and prevent accidental secret leakage.

prompts.title: Test Strategy Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need explicit test-scope reasoning and layered validation choices
prompts.avoid_when: the task is purely documentation or policy work with no behavior change
prompts.owner_intent: Make test scope intentional instead of defaulting to broad or shallow validation.

prompts.title: Tooling And Lint Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need validation-order discipline and project-tool alignment
prompts.avoid_when: the task has no applicable repo tooling
prompts.owner_intent: Use tooling as evidence and avoid repo-wide churn.

prompts.title: Adversarial Testing Prompt
prompts.use_when: you want break-it thinking for risky or exposed changes
prompts.avoid_when: the task is too small or low-risk to justify adversarial review overhead
prompts.owner_intent: Counter happy-path bias by forcing deliberate attempts to break the work.

prompts.title: Reliability And Recovery Prompt
prompts.use_when: restart safety, retries, rollback, or partial failure matter
prompts.avoid_when: the task has no meaningful failure-recovery dimension
prompts.owner_intent: Surface operational resilience concerns before shipping.

prompts.title: Security Review Prompt
prompts.use_when: trust boundaries, auth, secrets, or exposure risk are central
prompts.avoid_when: the change is clearly internal and low-risk
prompts.owner_intent: Demand explicit security reasoning instead of vague confidence.

prompts.title: Ship Readiness Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need an evidence-based go/no-go judgment
prompts.avoid_when: the work is exploratory and not near a ship decision
prompts.owner_intent: Prevent green-check theater by forcing explicit readiness criteria.

prompts.title: Compatibility And Migration Prompt
prompts.use_when: interfaces, configs, schemas, or workflows might break consumers
prompts.avoid_when: there is no downstream consumer or migration impact
prompts.owner_intent: Force migration and compatibility thinking before changes spread damage downstream.

prompts.title: Project Setup Prompt
prompts.use_when: the objective is initial setup or environment bring-up
prompts.avoid_when: the project is already running and the task is a bug fix or feature
prompts.owner_intent: Drive deliberate setup work without broad environment cleanup drift.

prompts.title: Project Setup Prompt Short
prompts.use_when: you need a lighter-weight setup prompt than the full setup version
prompts.avoid_when: the setup task needs full constraints and verification detail
prompts.owner_intent: Provide a compact setup variant for lower-friction startup tasks.

prompts.title: Context-Efficient Bug Fix Prompt
prompts.use_when: the objective is diagnosing and fixing a bug with minimal scope
prompts.avoid_when: the task is feature development or broad refactoring
prompts.owner_intent: Bias toward narrow, targeted bug fixes and clear verification.

prompts.title: Feature Work Prompt
prompts.use_when: the objective is adding or extending product behavior
prompts.avoid_when: the work is primarily debugging or review-only
prompts.owner_intent: Keep feature work scoped, validated, and aligned with local patterns.

prompts.title: Code Review Prompt
prompts.use_when: the task is reviewing an existing change set
prompts.avoid_when: you are expected to implement rather than review
prompts.owner_intent: Keep review findings concrete, prioritized, and evidence-based.

prompts.title: Refactor Prompt
prompts.use_when: the work is improving structure without changing intended behavior
prompts.avoid_when: the task is actually a feature or bug fix hiding behind a refactor label
prompts.owner_intent: Keep refactors behavior-preserving, reviewable, and justified.

prompts.title: Debugging Handoff Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need to hand off a debugging thread cleanly to another session or agent
prompts.avoid_when: there is no active debugging context to transfer
prompts.owner_intent: Capture the minimum debugging context needed for a clean restart.

prompts.title: Dependency Install Prompt
prompts.use_when: you need guidance for installing or upgrading dependencies safely
prompts.avoid_when: the task does not touch dependency installation
prompts.owner_intent: Keep dependency changes explicit, minimal, and verified.

prompts.title: Environment Constraints Prompt
prompts.use_when: the task depends on platform, runtime, or environment constraints
prompts.avoid_when: environment details do not materially affect the work
prompts.owner_intent: Prevent unnecessary setup churn and respect the repo's intended runtime.

prompts.title: Documentation Standards Prompt
prompts.use_when: the task is writing or reviewing operational repository documentation
prompts.avoid_when: the work is portfolio or GitHub Pages presentation rather than operational docs
prompts.owner_intent: Keep documentation operational, scannable, and useful for maintainers.

prompts.title: README Authoring Prompt
prompts.use_when: the task specifically targets a repository README
prompts.avoid_when: the work is non-README documentation or public portfolio presentation
prompts.owner_intent: Specialize README guidance without bloating the general docs prompt.

prompts.title: Legal Disclaimer Prompt
prompts.use_when: a repository needs the standard disclaimer wording
prompts.avoid_when: a repo-specific legal exception has been explicitly approved
prompts.owner_intent: Enforce one exact disclaimer phrase across repositories unless an explicit exception exists.

prompts.title: GitHub Pages Repo Style Prompt
prompts.use_when: the repo is a GitHub Pages or public-facing presentation site
prompts.avoid_when: the repo is internal or operational rather than presentation-focused
prompts.owner_intent: Provide public-site style rules without contaminating operational repo docs.

prompts.title: Portfolio Project Page Prompt
prompts.use_when: the work is a hiring-facing project page or portfolio artifact
prompts.avoid_when: the repo documentation should remain purely operational
prompts.owner_intent: Allow presentation-focused writing only where portfolio goals explicitly justify it.
